tactical_shape: Laboured to create against an ultra-defensive Ghana side
Chased the winner late, with bench impact from finishers
standout_players: Declan Rice 7/10 — back to his marauding best early on
Marc Guéhi 6/10 — range of passing adds so much
Reece James 6/10 — bright start before fading badly after lengthy treatment
Noni Madueke 6/10 — caused Ghana problems with directness
Jude Bellingham 6/10 — effort unimpeachable but lacked cutting edge
Harry Kane 6/10 — only eight touches in a quiet first half; should have buried late rebound
Bukayo Saka 7/10 — finally injected some urgency off the bench
Nico O’Reilly 6/10 — unlucky not to score with a header
Anthony Gordon 4/10 — little of substance, place under pressure
fatigue_injuries: Reece James needed lengthy treatment after being clattered by Jordan Ayew
Harry Kane noted as unusually quiet in a game where England laboured
England dropped off after the first 20 minutes
home_read.momentum: A reality check for England: the momentum from the Croatia win stalled, and the group remains in the balance after another spluttered game two.

tactical_shape: Marshalled midfield and defended deep, then looked dangerous on the break through substitutes
standout_players: Jerome Opoku 8/10 — giant defender, dominant in the air and on the ball
Jonas Adjetey 8/10 — organiser in chief of the defence
Thomas Partey 7/10 — marshalled midfield superbly and kept Kane quiet
Caleb Yirenkyi 7/10 — another impressive outing in midfield
Kwasi Sibo 7/10 — ran his socks off and made a crucial block
Antoine Semenyo 7/10 — clear England were wary of his threat
Jordan Ayew 7/10 — put himself about and caused problems
Gideon Mensah 7/10 — settled after early struggles
Benjamin Asare 6/10 — didn’t have much to do
Fatawu 6/10 — always a danger on the break
Adu 7/10 — caused England’s defence real issues after coming on
away_read.momentum: Ghana came away with a stubborn, encouraging draw and left the group finely poised, with their defensive resistance earning real respect.
